title: An example with attributes associated with ontology terms

attributes.title: Topics
attributes.description: Topics, used for annotating. Describes the domain, field of interest, of study, application, work, data, or technology. Initially seeded from the EDAM ontology.

attributes.title: Operations
attributes.description: Operations, used for annotating. Describes a function that can take place over some inputs to produce an output. Initially seeded from the EDAM ontology

attributes.title: Data Formats
attributes.description: Data formats, used for annotating. Describes a defined way or layout of representing and structuring data in a computer file, blob, string, message, or elsewhere. Initially seeded from the EDAM ontology.

attributes.title: Data types
attributes.description: Data types, used for annotating. Describes information that is that can be processed by dedicated computational tools that can use the data as input or produce it as output. Initially seeded from the EDAM ontology
